pattern: Greedily extend an existing run with each number when possible, otherwise start a fresh run that must reserve the next two consecutive values.
hints: Process numbers in sorted order and decide for each copy whether it lengthens a chain that already ends one below it, or begins a new chain.
Track how many chains currently end at each value so you can prefer appending to an open chain before opening a new one.
A new chain is only valid if you can also claim the next two consecutive values; if neither appending nor opening works, the split is impossible.

edge_inputs.description: one long run plus one short run

stress.description: concatenated consecutive runs of length >= 3, shuffled by sort
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = []
    target = rng.randint(1, max(3, size))
    while len(nums) < target:
        start = rng.randint(-1000, 990)
        length = rng.randint(3, 8)
        for i in range(length):
            nums.append(start + i)
    nums = nums[:target]
    nums.sort()
    return [nums]
stress.note: mostly splittable but truncation can break runs
